output: normal-This sequence is normal because it shows routine process and system-management activity without any unrecoverable hardware or kernel fault indicators. Although there are application-level terminations such as 'program <*> terminated with signal <*>' and 'kernel: task <*> exited with status <*>', these are followed by orderly operational events like 'RAS KERNEL INFO process <*> on node <*> ended', filesystem mount/unmount activity, network and heartbeat confirmation, daemon startup, and 'job <*> cleanup completed'. There are no panic messages, machine checks, unrecoverable interrupts, parity faults, TLB fatal errors, power deactivation events, or repeated failure escalations. The pattern is consistent with normal job completion and service housekeeping rather than a system failure.

input: I/O node booted successfully,<*> mounted /bluegene/<*> filesystem,<*> kernel: BGL torus link self-test passed,<*> tree network interface initialized,<*> environmental monitor: node card <*> temperature within range,<*> ddr errors(s) detected and corrected on rank <*>, symbol <*> bit <*>,total of <*> ddr error(s) detected and corrected,<*> torus receiver <*> input pipe error(s) (dcr <*>) detected and corrected,<*> L3 <*> error(s) (dcr <*>) detected and corrected,job <*> loaded on partition <*>,I/O link heartbeat received from <*> ,service action record cleared for node <*>,<*> tree receiver <*> in re-synch state event(s) (dcr <*>) detected,<*> tree receiver <*> in re-synch state event(s) (dcr <*>) detected,collective network CRC check passed,compute node <*> completed barrier synchronization,job <*> checkpoint completed,environmental monitor: fan speed nominal on rack <*>,<*> ddr errors(s) detected and corrected on rank <*>, symbol <*> bit <*>,total of <*> ddr error(s) detected and corrected,job <*> completed normally,partition <*> returned to idle state,
output: normal-This sequence is normal because it shows a healthy boot, initialization, job execution, and clean completion pattern without any unrecoverable fault indicators. The early events such as "I/O node booted successfully," "<*> mounted /bluegene/<*> filesystem," "<*> kernel: BGL torus link self-test passed," and "<*> tree network interface initialized," indicate successful startup of core BGL subsystems. Runtime health is reinforced by "environmental monitor: node card <*> temperature within range," "environmental monitor: fan speed nominal on rack <*>," and "I/O link heartbeat received from <*> ," which are all consistent with stable operation. The sequence does include correctable hardware events, including "<*> ddr errors(s) detected and corrected on rank <*>, symbol <*> bit <*>," "total of <*> ddr error(s) detected and corrected," "<*> torus receiver <*> input pipe error(s) (dcr <*>) detected and corrected," and "<*> L3 <*> error(s) (dcr <*>) detected and corrected," but these are explicitly corrected and therefore consistent with normal BGL behavior. The two "<*> tree receiver <*> in re-synch state event(s) (dcr <*>) detected," messages are transient fabric-state notifications and are not accompanied by panic, machine check, kernel termination, unrecoverable interrupt, link failure, or node deactivation events. Most importantly, the workload proceeds successfully through "job <*> loaded on partition <*>," "compute node <*> completed barrier synchronization," and "job <*> checkpoint completed," then ends with "job <*> completed normally," followed by "partition <*> returned to idle state," confirming that the system remained operational and recovered from any corrected faults without escalation.
